headline: AI models nail tech rally while Claude catastrophically misreads energy sector
summary: Models showed strong consensus on tech strength with SPY and NVDA calls proving highly accurate, while disagreement on TSLA saw bears get burned as it surged. Claude's XLE prediction stands out as the day's biggest disaster, expecting $95.50 but seeing the stock crash to $57.63, suggesting a major data error or market shock.

consensus_picks.note: Four models correctly called SPY's rise to 742.31, while only Claude went bearish and paid for it.
Perfect consensus on NVDA's continued AI-driven momentum, with all three models scoring 1.2.
Three models expected TSLA weakness around $428, but the stock rallied to $445.27, with only Claude and Gemini calling it right.

best_call.summary: Perfectly nailed NVDA's close at $225.83 with 70% confidence, capitalizing on AI semiconductor momentum.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence call for XLE at $95.50 crashed spectacularly as the energy ETF closed at $57.63, a 40% miss suggesting major data issues.
